merger_name: Life Without Barriers – Mallacoota District Health & Support Service

targets.name: MALLACOOTA DISTRICT HEALTH & SUPPORT SERVICE INC

other_parties.name: Family and Youth Therapeutic Services Limited

merger_description:
Life Without Barriers (**Life Without Barriers**), an ACNC-registered charity incorporated as a public company limited by guarantee, proposes to merge with Mallacoota District Health & Support Services Inc. (**MDHSS**), an ACNC registered charity incorporated as a Victorian incorporated association. By way of a Merger Implementation Deed, Life Without Barriers, together with five Life Without Barriers nominees (comprising directors and/or executives of Life Without Barriers), propose to become the only members of MDHSS, replacing all current members of MDHSS (the **Acquisition**).

Life Without Barriers is a not-for-profit organisation that provides comprehensive social services to communities across Australia. Relevantly, it is an NDIS-registered provider of disability services. It also provides in-home aged care services, providing independent living and behavioural support to elderly clients and persons with disabilities. These services are provided in various locations throughout Australia, however Life Without Barriers does not offer services within the East Gippsland region of Victoria (where MDHSS operates).

MDHSS is an ACNC-registered charity that provides primary and allied health, aged care, disability and community services in Mallacoota, Victoria and surrounding areas. Relevantly, MDHSS’ aged care services offering includes both in-home care for elderly clients, as well as operating independent living units and a respite care centre. As an unregistered disability support provider, its disability services are limited to a variety of everyday disability supports, provided the participant’s funding is self-managed or plan-managed.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Life Without Barriers applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of Mallacoota District Health & Support
Services Inc. (MDHSS), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

Life Without Barriers, is a not-for-profit organisation that provides
comprehensive social services to communities across Australia.
Relevantly, it is an NDIS-registered provider of disability services. It
also provides in-home aged care services, providing independent
living and behavioural support to elderly clients and persons with
disabilities. These services are provided in various locations
throughout Australia.
MDHSS, provides primary and allied health, aged care, disability and
community services in Mallacoota and surrounding areas.
Relevantly, MDHSS’ aged care services offering includes both in-
home care for elderly clients, as well as operating independent living
units and a respite care centre. As an unregistered disability support
provider, its disability services are limited to a variety of everyday
disability supports, provided the participant’s funding is self-
managed or plan-managed.
MDHSS also operates a small medical centre in Mallacoota from
which primary and allied health services are provided to Mallacoota
residents. Many of these services (including podiatry, physiotherapy,
pathology, dentistry and psychology) are supplied by independent
contractors operating within MDHSS’ medical centre.
Mallacoota is a town in the East Gippsland region in Victoria,
Australia. Life Without Barriers does not offer services within the
East Gippsland region of Victoria.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between Life Without Barriers
and MDHSS in the supply of in-home aged care and disability
services in Mallacoota and surrounding areas and a small
horizontal overlap in the wider regional area
b. there are alternative suppliers of in-home aged care and
disability services in Mallacoota and surrounding areas.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
